lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣੁ ਨ ਸੁਝਈ ਨਾ ਸੁਝੈ ਦਰਬਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: man aavan jaan na sujhee naa sujhai darabaar |
lines.english: The mortal being does not understand the comings and goings of reincarnation; he does not see the Court of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕੀ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਬੇਨਤੀ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਵੈ ਬਖਸਿ ਮਿਲਾਇ ॥੪੧॥
lines.translit: naanak kee prabh benatee har bhaavai bakhas milaae |41|
lines.english: Nanak offers this prayer: O Lord God, please forgive me, and unite me with Yourself. ||41||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹਿ ਪਲੇਟਿਆ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਅਗਿਆਨੁ ਗੁਬਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: maaeaa mohi palettiaa antar agiaan gubaar |
lines.english: He is wrapped up in emotional attachment and Maya, and within his being is the darkness of ignorance.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਬ ਨਰੁ ਸੁਤਾ ਜਾਗਿਆ ਸਿਰਿ ਡੰਡੁ ਲਗਾ ਬਹੁ ਭਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: tab nar sutaa jaagiaa sir ddandd lagaa bahu bhaar |
lines.english: The sleeping person wakes, only when he is hit on the head by a heavy club.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਾਂ ਕਰਾਂ ਉਪਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਚੇਤਿਆ ਸੇ ਪਾਇਨਿ ਮੋਖ ਦੁਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: guramukhaan karaan upar har chetiaa se paaein mokh duaar |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs dwell upon the Lord; they find the door of salvation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਆਪਿ ਓਹਿ ਉਧਰੇ ਸਭ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਤਰੇ ਪਰਵਾਰ ॥੪੨॥
lines.translit: naanak aap ohi udhare sabh kuttanb tare paravaar |42|
lines.english: O Nanak, they themselves are saved, and all their relatives are carried across as well. ||42||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਹਰਿ ਰਸਿ ਧ੍ਰਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee har ras dhraapai |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, the mortal is satisfied by the sublime essence of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦਿ ਮਰੈ ਸੋ ਮੁਆ ਜਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: sabad marai so muaa jaapai |
lines.english: Whoever dies in the Word of the Shabad, is known to be truly dead.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਬਦੈ ਮੁਆ ਹੈ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: bin sabadai muaa hai sabh koe |
lines.english: Without the Shabad, everyone is dead.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਮੁਆ ਅਪੁਨਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਖੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh muaa apunaa janam khoe |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh dies; his life is wasted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਦਰਗਹਿ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਿਞਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: har darageh gur sabad siyaapai |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, he is recognized in the Court of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕਰਤਾ ਕਰੇ ਸੁ ਹੋਇ ॥੪੩॥
lines.translit: naanak karataa kare su hoe |43|
lines.english: O Nanak, whatever the Creator Lord does, comes to pass. ||43||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਚੇਤਹਿ ਅੰਤਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਰੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: har naam na cheteh ant dukh roe |
lines.english: Those who do not remember the Name of the Lord, shall cry in pain in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੁਢੇ ਕਦੇ ਨਾਹੀ ਜਿਨੑਾ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗਿਆਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh budte kade naahee jinaa antar surat giaan |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs never grow old; within them is intuitive understanding and spiritual wisdom.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਸਦਾ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਰਵਹਿ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਹਜ ਧਿਆਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa sadaa har gun raveh antar sehaj dhiaan |
lines.english: They chant the Praises of the Lord, forever and ever; deep within, they intuitively meditate on the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਇ ਸਦਾ ਅਨੰਦਿ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਰਹਹਿ ਦੁਖਿ ਸੁਖਿ ਏਕ ਸਮਾਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: oe sadaa anand bibek raheh dukh sukh ek samaan |
lines.english: They dwell forever in blissful knowledge of the Lord; they look upon pain and pleasure as one and the same.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨਾ ਨਦਰੀ ਇਕੋ ਆਇਆ ਸਭੁ ਆਤਮ ਰਾਮੁ ਪਛਾਨੁ ॥੪੪॥
lines.translit: tinaa nadaree iko aaeaa sabh aatam raam pachhaan |44|
lines.english: They see the One Lord in all, and realize the Lord, the Supreme Soul of all. ||44||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਚਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਵਦੇ ਸਭ ਧਰਮ ਰਾਇ ਕੈ ਜਾਂਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: vich haumai karam kamaavade sabh dharam raae kai jaanhee |
lines.english: They do all their deeds in egotism, and they must answer to the Righteous Judge of Dharma.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਬਾਲਕੁ ਬਿਰਧਿ ਸਮਾਨਿ ਹੈ ਜਿਨੑਾ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh baalak biradh samaan hai jinaa antar har surat naahee |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs are like stupid children; they do not keep the Lord in their thoughts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਛੇ ਨਿਰਮਲੇ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸੁਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh hachhe niramale gur kai sabad subhaae |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs are good and immaculately pure; they are embellished and exalted with the Word of the Guru's Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨਾ ਮੈਲੁ ਪਤੰਗੁ ਨ ਲਗਈ ਜਿ ਚਲਨਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: onaa mail patang na lagee ji chalan satigur bhaae |
lines.english: Not even a tiny bit of filth sticks to them; they walk in harmony with the Will of the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਜੂਠਿ ਨ ਉਤਰੈ ਜੇ ਸਉ ਧੋਵਣ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh jootth na utarai je sau dhovan paae |
lines.english: The filth of the manmukhs is not washed away, even if they wash hundreds of times.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮੇਲਿਅਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਅੰਕਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੪੫॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh melian gur kai ank samaae |45|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukhs are united with the Lord; they merge into the Guru's Being. ||45||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਕਮਲਾ ਰਗੜੈ ਲੁਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh kamalaa ragarrai lujhai |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh drives himself crazy with worries and stubborn struggles.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੁਰਾ ਕਰੇ ਸੁ ਕੇਹਾ ਸਿਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: buraa kare su kehaa sijhai |
lines.english: How can someone do bad things, and still live with himself?

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣੈ ਰੋਹਿ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਦਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: aapanai rohi aape hee dajhai |
lines.english: By his own anger, he only burns himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਇ ਤਿਸੁ ਸਭ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੁਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh hoe tis sabh kichh sujhai |
lines.english: But those who become Gurmukh understand everything.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨ ਸਿਉ ਲੁਝੈ ॥੪੬॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh man siau lujhai |46|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh struggles with his own mind. ||46||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨ ਸੇਵਿਓ ਸਬਦਿ ਨ ਕੀਤੋ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jinaa satigur purakh na sevio sabad na keeto veechaar |
lines.english: Those who do not serve the True Guru, the Primal Being, and do not reflect upon the Word of the Shabad

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਇ ਮਾਣਸ ਜੂਨਿ ਨ ਆਖੀਅਨਿ ਪਸੂ ਢੋਰ ਗਾਵਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: oe maanas joon na aakheean pasoo dtor gaavaar |
lines.english: - do not call them human beings; they are just animals and stupid beasts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨਾ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਨ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਸਉ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਨ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: onaa antar giaan na dhiaan hai har sau preet na piaar |
lines.english: They have no spiritual wisdom or meditation within their beings; they are not in love with the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਮੁਏ ਵਿਕਾਰ ਮਹਿ ਮਰਿ ਜੰਮਹਿ ਵਾਰੋ ਵਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh mue vikaar meh mar jameh vaaro vaar |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs die in evil and corruption; they die and are reborn, again and again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੋਹਣੇ ਤਿਤੁ ਸਚੈ ਦਰਬਾਰਿ ॥੪੭॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh sohane tith sachai darabaar |47|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukhs look beautiful in that Court of the True Lord. ||47||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਵਦਿਆ ਨੋ ਮਿਲੈ ਸੁ ਜੀਵਦੇ ਹਰਿ ਜਗਜੀਵਨ ਉਰ ਧਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: jeevadiaa no milai su jeevade har jagajeevan ur dhaar |
lines.english: They alone live, who join with the living; enshrine the Lord, the Lord of Life, within your heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਮੰਦਰੁ ਹਰਿ ਸਾਜਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਵਸੈ ਜਿਸੁ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: har mandar har saajiaa har vasai jis naal |
lines.english: The Lord built the Harimandir, the Temple of the Lord; the Lord dwells within it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਹਰਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ ਪਰਜਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: guramatee har paaeaa maaeaa moh parajaal |
lines.english: Following the Guru's Teachings, I have found the Lord; my emotional attachment to Maya has been burnt away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਭਗਵੰਤੀ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਜਿਨਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਲਧਾ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: dhan bhagavantee naanakaa jinaa guramukh ladhaa har bhaal |
lines.english: Blessed is that happy soul-bride, O Nanak, who, as Gurmukh, seeks and finds the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਮੰਦਰਿ ਵਸਤੁ ਅਨੇਕ ਹੈ ਨਵ ਨਿਧਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਮਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: har mandar vasat anek hai nav nidh naam samaal |
lines.english: Countless things are in the Harimandir, the Temple of the Lord; contemplate the Naam, and the nine treasures will be yours.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਭਾਗੀ ਗੜ ਮੰਦਰੁ ਖੋਜਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਪਾਇਆ ਨਾਲਿ ॥੪੮॥
lines.translit: vaddabhaagee garr mandar khojiaa har hiradai paaeaa naal |48|
lines.english: By great good fortune, one searches the temple of the body-fortress, and finds the Lord within the heart. ||48||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਦਹ ਦਿਸਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਰਹੇ ਅਤਿ ਤਿਸਨਾ ਲੋਭ ਵਿਕਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh deh dis fir rahe at tisanaa lobh vikaar |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukhs wander lost in the ten directions, led by intense desire, greed and corruption.
